Title: The tomato cytochrome P450 CYP712G1 catalyses the double oxidation of orobanchol en route to the rhizosphere signalling strigolactone, solanacol

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses double oxidation of orobanchol, likely via hydroxy-orobanchol intermediate(s), to form three didehydro-orobanchol (DDH) isomers.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor(s) en route to solanacol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for conversion of orobanchol to DDH; required branch step toward solanacol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Rhizosphere signalling molecule and phytohormone; likely involved in rhizosphere communication.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of orobanchol into DDH isomers and thereby supports solanacol biosynthesis; silencing causes strong orobanchol accumulation and reduced DDH/solanacol.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Solanum lycopersicum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed; induced by phosphate starvation, repressed by phosphate replenishment, upregulated by GR24; expression pattern similar to known strigolactone biosynthetic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In tomato roots under phosphate starvation; assessed in tomato root exudate phenotype, transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves, and heterologously expressed in yeast microsomes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-expression analysis; transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with reconstructed orobanchol pathway; VIGS in tomato; yeast microsome in vitro assay; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; protein modelling and substrate docking; site-directed mutagenesis (D305A, D312A).
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; phosphate replenishment; GR24 application under phosphate starvation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth or yield penalty reported; silencing triggered feedback changes in upstream SL biosynthetic gene expression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modify tomato root-exudate strigolactone composition, especially DDH isomers and solanacol, for rhizosphere trait engineering and breeding.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One DDH isomer, likely DDH3, is proposed to be the substrate for solanacol formation, but CYP712G1 does not complete DDH-to-solanacol conversion; another enzyme is likely required. CYP712G1 also accepts 2'-epi-orobanchol with lower efficiency. Asp305 is essential and Asp312 strongly contributes to activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP712G1 was identified by co-expression with known tomato strigolactone biosynthetic genes under phosphate starvation and GR24-responsive conditions. 2. In Nicotiana benthamiana and yeast microsomes, CYP712G1 consumed orobanchol and produced three DDH isomers, likely via hydroxy-orobanchol intermediate(s). 3. VIGS of CYP712G1 in tomato caused a 12.2-fold increase in orobanchol and decreased DDH and solanacol, demonstrating in planta function in the orobanchol-to-solanacol branch.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Core strigolactone biosynthetic enzyme used here as positive-control bait and VIGS control for pathway perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing reduced orobanchol, DDH isomers, and solanacol in tomato root exudate, validating the assay system.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by phosphate starvation; repressed by phosphate replenishment; upregulated by GR24 under phosphate starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tomato roots; used in transient pathway reconstruction in N. benthamiana and as VIGS positive control in tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS in tomato; RT-qPCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; use in transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphate starvation; phosphate replenishment; GR24 application
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing caused feedback upregulation of SlCCD7, SlMAX1 and SlCYP712G1 and reduced SlD27 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as positive control and bait gene; not the main discovery target of the study.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCCD8 served as a validated positive control for strigolactone pathway perturbation. 2. VIGS of SlCCD8 strongly reduced strigolactones in tomato root exudate. 3. SlCCD8 expression and pathway responses supported the RNA-seq/co-expression strategy used to identify CYP712G1.
